headline: Jobs growth collapses to 57K in June
detail: This is well below expectations and signals the labor market is genuinely softening. For tech and semis, slowing growth hurts revenue outlooks even if rate-cut hopes provide a short-term bid - it's a double-edged print. On margin, a risk-off reversal triggered by growth fear could hit SOXL and SPAL especially hard given their leverage.
rec: No new buys off this - the rate-cut tailwind is already in prices for QQQ/SPY. Watch SOXL closely; if semis roll over on growth fear, a 3x leveraged position on margin has very little room before serious damage. No scanner setup aligns with a new buy here right now.

headline: Labor force participation hits 50-year low (ex-Covid)
detail: Fewer people working or looking for work is a structural drag on consumer spending, which eventually feeds through to enterprise tech budgets and chip demand. This compounds the weak payrolls number and is a slow-burn negative for your chip-heavy book. Not an immediate trigger but worsens the macro backdrop for SOXL and SMH.
rec: Nothing to trade directly off this headline, but it reinforces keeping SOXL size in check given the macro headwind building under chip names. No scanner setup provides a compelling new entry here.

headline: Stock market gains created nearly 1M new millionaires in 2025
detail: This is a sentiment/momentum data point - wealth effect is real and supports continued retail inflows into large-cap tech and index ETFs like QQQ and SPY. Mildly supportive for your HOLD positions. Does not change the near-term picture for your distressed names.
rec: Your QQQ and SPY are fine and already held - stops at 670 and 705 respectively give reasonable room. No reason to add or trim based on this headline alone.

headline: Jersey Mike's files for IPO with 50% same-store sales growth
detail: Not directly relevant to tech or chips. Signals IPO market is reopening which can be a mild risk-on indicator, but this is a consumer/restaurant name with no overlap to your book. Negligible signal for your portfolio.
rec: Nothing here touches your holdings or sectors - skip it entirely.
